content.problemStatement:
Now, we will implement user registration, login, and logout. Allow logged-in users to post top-level comments on a page. \
**Outcome:**
- After this task, your website will have a functional user system.
- Users can create a new account via a signup page.
- Users can log in and log out. The top of the page will show their status.
- When a user is logged in and views a page, a comment form will appear.
- Submitting the form will post a new comment, which will then be displayed at the top of the comments list on that page.
### Files to Create/Modify for this Task:
- comment_system/settings.py (Add the following URLs)
   - LOGIN_REDIRECT_URL = 'comments:homepage'
   - LOGOUT_REDIRECT_URL = 'comments:homepage'
   - LOGIN_URL = 'login'
- comment_system/urls.py 
   - Include Django's built-in authentication URLs (for login/logout) and your new signup URL.
- comments/models.py 
   - Add the Comment model. It needs a relationship to both the User and the Page.
- comments/forms.py 
   - This form will be used to create new comments.
- comments/views.py **(Done for you)**
   - Add the `signup` and `add_comment` views. 
   - Also, update page_detail to pass the form and the list of comments to the template.
- comments/urls.py 
   - Add a URL pattern for the add_comment view.
- comments/templates/comments/base.html **(Done for you)**
   - Update the header to show the user's login status and display messages from the Django messages framework.
- comments/templates/comments/page_detail.html **(Done for you)**
   - Update the template to loop through and display comments, and to show the comment form for logged-in users.
- comments/templates/registration/login.html **(Done for you)**
   - This file is created inside the comments/templates/registration/ directory. Django automatically looks for it here.
- comments/templates/registration/signup.html **(Done for you)**
   - This file is created inside the comments/templates/registration/ directory.

**After completing the above**
- Click on **RUN** and open the browser on a new tab.
- Test the new features:
   - Go to /accounts/signup/ to create a new user account.
   - Log out and log back in at /accounts/login/.
   - Navigate to a page and post a comment. Verify that it appears.
